rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS ALAN GREENBERG VS. ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 23, 2025 line 1. RESPONDENT ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO AMENED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED. Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for tax refund for 2021-2024 is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to allege exhaustion of administrative remedies in accordance with the procedure outlined in Defendant's moving papers and failed to provide any indication that Plaintiff may in good faith amend the Petition to allege compliance. Allegations in paragraph 48 and 49 show that Plaintiff did not comply with the procedure, even though Plaintiff makes conclusory allegations that Plaintiff did so. Demurrer to the causes of action for mandamus is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to assert a claim for tax refund for the year 2020 and name CCSF as a defendant on that cause of action. Petitioner sufficiently stated facts showing that the claim related to tax year 2020 was timely made after the issuance of the findings of fact. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CHIZINSKI ET AL VS. APHRM CORPORATION, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 23, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT S4 DEVELOPMENTS, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion is granted as to the prayer for tax neutralization for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting award of these damages in line with the case cited by Plaintiff in opposition. ("to offset the increased tax burden on plaintiff resulting from a lump sum award of damages as compared to what plaintiff would have owed in taxes if the earnings had been received sequentially each year." Economy v. Sutter (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 1147). Motion is otherwise denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). DEFENDANT AJA MCKINNEY, ALVIN WILLIAMS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend on uncertainty grounds. Plaintiff chose to use optional UD form complaint. In section 7 thereof Plaintiff stated "specific subpart supporting why tenancy is exempt," but provided no such subpart rendering the complaint uncertain. Plaintiff may amend the complaint by filling out all the prompts of the form complaint or using pleading format. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
